---
title: "nitrix.geometry.deformation"
generated: true
signature-hash: f712fc46cad8efeb
description: "Deformation- and velocity-field algebra."
listing:
  id: nitrix-geometry-deformation-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-geometry-deformation/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.geometry.deformation`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Deformation- and velocity-field algebra.

The operations the diffeomorphic (log-Demons) recipe needs on top of the
stationary-velocity-field stack ([`integrate_velocity_field`](nitrix-geometry-grid/integrate_velocity_field.qmd)
is the exponential $\exp(v)$):

- [`compose_displacement`](nitrix-geometry-deformation/compose_displacement.qmd) -- compose two displacement fields,
  $(\mathrm{id} + u) \circ (\mathrm{id} + v)$, the warp-by-then-warp
  operation.
- [`compose_velocity`](nitrix-geometry-deformation/compose_velocity.qmd) -- the Baker-Campbell-Hausdorff approximation of
  the velocity whose exponential is $\exp(v) \circ \exp(u)$ (the
  log-domain update); first order is plain addition, second order adds half
  the Lie bracket.
- [`invert_displacement`](nitrix-geometry-deformation/invert_displacement.qmd) -- the inverse displacement, as the fixed
  point $s_{\mathrm{inv}} = -s \circ (\mathrm{id} + s_{\mathrm{inv}})$
  (via `fixed_point_solve`, so it is differentiable).
- [`field_log`](nitrix-geometry-deformation/field_log.qmd) -- the stationary-velocity *logarithm* of a deformation
  (the inverse of [`integrate_velocity_field`](nitrix-geometry-grid/integrate_velocity_field.qmd)), by
  inverse scaling-and-squaring; the dense analogue of
  `matrix_log`.  Recovers a stationary-velocity-field
  parameterisation of a deformation solved directly in the group (the greedy
  registration path).  The exponential is not surjective -- a general
  diffeomorphism (e.g. a greedy composition of many non-commuting warps) is
  *not* $\exp(v)$ for any single stationary $v$ -- so
  [`field_log`](nitrix-geometry-deformation/field_log.qmd) returns the *best stationary-velocity-field fit*: the
  round-trip $\exp(\mathrm{field\_log}(\varphi)) = \varphi$ is exact
  by construction, but [`field_log`](nitrix-geometry-deformation/field_log.qmd) is a true inverse of the exponential
  only on the stationary-velocity-field submanifold (a nonzero fit residual
  off it).

Channel-last fields `(*spatial, ndim)`; coordinates are in index space
(the [`identity_grid`](nitrix-geometry-grid/identity_grid.qmd) convention).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
| `ndim` | number of spatial dimensions |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-geometry-deformation-symbols}
:::
